    73→
    74→**Visual checks:**
    75→
    76→- ✅ Elements don't overlap
    77→- ✅ All elements visible and accessible
    78→- ✅ UI is usable (buttons clickable, forms submittable)
    79→
    80→**Responsive checks:**
    81→
    82→- ✅ Component works at mobile breakpoint (<960px)
    83→- ✅ Component works at desktop breakpoint (>=960px)
    84→- ✅ Animations correct on both breakpoints
    85→- ✅ No CSS specificity conflicts (mobile styles not overridden by global)
    86→- ✅ If requirement said "on mobile" - ALL variants tested on mobile
    87→
    88→### 2. Readability Review (Non-Blocking)
    89→
    90→After code review, check readability for human maintainers. This is non-blocking — issues go to suggestions, not bugs.
    91→
    92→**Naming check:**
    93→
    94→- [ ] Function names clearly describe what they do (verb + noun)
    95→- [ ] Variable names are descriptive (no single letters except `i`, `j` in loops)
    96→- [ ] Boolean variables have `is/has/can/should` prefix
    97→- [ ] Constants are SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE or grouped in objects
    98→- [ ] Types follow naming convention (T, DTO, SDTO, E suffixes)
    99→
   100→**Complexity check:**
   101→
   102→- [ ] No functions longer than 30 lines
   103→- [ ] No deeply nested code (max 3 levels of nesting)
   104→- [ ] Complex logic has explanatory WHY comments
   105→- [ ] No magic numbers or strings
   106→
   107→**Clarity check:**
   108→
   109→- [ ] Error messages are user-friendly (no technical jargon for users)
   110→- [ ] Code is self-documenting (no need for WHAT comments)
   111→- [ ] New developer could understand this code in 5 minutes
   112→
   113→Report issues in the "Readability Review" section of your output (see output format below).
   114→
   115→### 3. Write Unit Tests
   116→
   117→**MANDATORY - Must test:**
   118→
   119→- All services (Services/)
   120→- All utilities (Shared/utils)
   121→- All business logic
   122→
   123→**OPTIONAL - Test if complex:**
   124→
   125→- Components with complex logic
   126→- Custom hooks with logic
   127→- Form validation logic
   128→
   129→**Test location:** Place unit tests next to the file being tested:
   130→
   131→```
   132→src/services/authService.ts
   133→src/services/authService.test.ts  ← here
   134→```
   135→
   136→**Test framework:** Vitest + Testing Library
   137→
   138→**Test style:**
   139→
   140→```typescript
   141→import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
   142→import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react';
   143→
   144→describe('FunctionName or ComponentName', () => {
   145→  it('should do something specific', () => {
   146→    // Arrange
   147→    const input = 'test';
   148→
   149→    // Act
   150→    const result = functionToTest(input);
   151→
   152→    // Assert
   153→    expect(result).toBe('expected');
   154→  });
   155→});
   156→```
   157→
   158→**What to test:**
   159→
   160→- Happy path (normal flow)
   161→- Edge cases (empty, null, undefined)
   162→- Error cases (API errors, validation errors)
   163→- Business logic rules (from BUSINESS_RULES.md)
   164→- State changes
   165→- API calls (mock with vi.fn())
   166→
   167→**Test code quality:**
   168→
   169→- Tests must compile without TypeScript errors
   170→- Mock functions typed correctly: `vi.fn() as unknown as () => void`
   171→- Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify test files
   172→
   173→### 4. Write E2E Tests
   174→
   175→**When to write E2E:**
   176→
   177→- Critical user flows (auth, checkout, core features)
   178→- Key feature implementations
   179→- User journeys that span multiple pages
   180→
   181→**Test location:** `client/tests/feature-name.spec.ts`
   182→
   183→**Test framework:** Playwright
   184→
   185→**E2E test style:**
   186→
   187→```typescript
   188→import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';
   189→
   190→test.describe('Feature Name', () => {
   191→  test('should complete user flow', async ({ page }) => {
   192→    await page.goto('/login');
   193→    await page.fill('[data-testid="email"]', 'user@example.com');
   194→    await page.fill('[data-testid="password"]', 'password123');
   195→    await page.click('[data-testid="submit"]');
   196→
   197→    await expect(page).toHaveURL('/dashboard');
   198→    await expect(page.locator('h1')).toContainText('Dashboard');
   199→  });
   200→});
   201→```
   202→
   203→### 5. Run Tests
   204→
   205→Execute all tests:
   206→
   207→```bash
   208→yarn test           # Run unit tests
   209→yarn test:e2e       # Run e2e tests
   210→```
   211→
   212→**Tests must pass:**
   213→
   214→- All unit tests green ✅
   215→- All e2e tests green ✅
   216→- No test failures
   217→
   218→**If tests fail:**
   219→
   220→- Mark as "Failed - Found bugs"
   221→- Document each failure in report
   222→
   223→### 6. Find and Report Bugs
   224→
   225→Check for:
   226→
   227→- Code doesn't match requirements
   228→- Acceptance criteria not met
   229→- TypeScript errors
   230→- Runtime errors
   231→- Logic errors
   232→- Missing error handling
   233→- UI issues (overlapping, not visible)
   234→- Accessibility issues
   235→- Performance problems (if obvious)
   236→
   237→**Bug report format:**
   238→
   239→```markdown
   240→## Bug: [Short description]
   241→
   242→**Status:** NOT FIXED
   243→**Severity:** Critical / High / Medium / Low
   244→**File:** src/path/to/file.tsx
   245→**Line:** 45
   246→**Description:** [What's wrong and why it's a problem]
   247→**Expected behavior:** [What should happen]
   248→**Actual behavior:** [What actually happens]
   249→**Steps to reproduce:**
   250→
   251→1. Step one
   252→2. Step two
   253→3. Bug appears
   254→
   255→**Suggested fix:** [If you have an idea how to fix]
   256→```
   257→
   258→After Developer fixes, update status to `FIXED` or keep as `NOT FIXED`.
   259→
   260→### 7. Iteration Limit
   261→
   262→**Maximum 2 iterations:**
   263→
   264→- Iteration 1: Find bugs → report → Developer fixes → re-check
   265→- Iteration 2: Find bugs → report → Developer fixes → re-check
   266→
   267→**After 2 iterations:** If bugs still remain, mark as "Needs Review" and escalate.
   268→
   269→## Output Format
   270→
   271→Save output to: `tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md`
   272→
   273→**First line must be status:**
   274→
   275→```
   276→Status: Done
   277→```
   278→
   279→or
   280→
   281→```
   282→Status: Failed - Found bugs (iteration 1/2)
   283→```
   284→
   285→or
   286→
   287→```
   288→Status: Needs Review - [reason]
   289→```
   290→
   291→**Then provide:**
   292→
   293→```markdown
   294→# Testing Report for [Task Name]
   295→
   296→## Code Review Results
   297→
   298→### Standards Compliance
   299→
   300→✅ No forbidden items (any, interface, function)
   301→✅ TypeScript naming correct (DTO/SDTO/T/E)
   302→✅ Props named PropsT
   303→✅ Using const everywhere
   304→✅ Path aliases used correctly
   305→✅ Memoization applied
   306→✅ SCSS mobile-first
   307→
   308→### Quality Checks
   309→
   310→✅ Matches Developer's approach
   311→✅ Acceptance criteria fulfilled
   312→✅ Error handling present
   313→✅ Accessibility implemented
   314→
   315→### Visual Checks
   316→
   317→✅ No overlapping elements
   318→✅ All elements visible
   319→✅ UI is functional
   320→
   321→## Test Coverage
   322→
   323→### Unit Tests Written
   324→
   325→- ✅ `src/services/authService.test.ts` - 8 tests, all passing
   326→- ✅ `Shared/utils/validation.test.ts` - 12 tests, all passing
   327→
   328→**Coverage summary:**
   329→
   330→- Services: 100% (all services tested) ✅
   331→- Utils: 100% (all utils tested) ✅
   332→- Business logic: covered ✅
   333→
   334→### E2E Tests Written
   335→
   336→- ✅ `client/tests/auth.spec.ts` - Login/logout flow
   337→
   338→### Test Execution Results
   339→```
   340→
   341→yarn test
   342→✅ All unit tests passed (25/25)
   343→
   344→yarn test:e2e
   345→✅ All e2e tests passed (8/8)
   346→
   347→```
   348→
   349→## Bugs Found
   350→
   351→### Bug 1: [Title]
   352→**Severity:** High
   353→**File:** src/services/authService.ts
   354→**Line:** 23
   355→**Description:** [Detailed description]
   356→**Expected:** [expected behavior]
   357→**Actual:** [actual behavior]
   358→**Steps to reproduce:**
   359→1. Step one
   360→2. Step two
   361→**Suggested fix:** [If you have an idea]
   362→
   363→## Acceptance Criteria Check
   364→
   365→From BA requirements:
   366→- ✅ User can login with email and password
   367→- ✅ Form shows validation errors
   368→- ✅ Loading state displayed during API call
   369→
   370→## Summary
   371→
   372→**Status:** [Done / Failed with X bugs / Needs Review]
   373→**Tests written:** X unit + Y e2e
   374→**Tests passing:** All / X failing
   375→**Bugs found:** X critical, Y high, Z medium
   376→**Iteration:** 1/2 or 2/2
   377→
   378→## Readability Review
   379→
   380→### Passed ✅
   381→- Clear function names
   382→- Good variable naming
   383→- No magic numbers
   384→
   385→### Suggestions (Non-Blocking)
   386→- `src/services/userService.ts:45` — function `process()` should be renamed to `processUserRegistration()`
   387→- `src/components/Form.tsx:23` — magic number `5` should be `MAX_RETRY_COUNT`
   388→
   389→## Refactoring Suggestions (Non-Blocking)
   390→
   391→These suggestions are for future improvements and do NOT block this task:
   392→- [Suggestion 1]
   393→- [Suggestion 2]
   394→```
   395→
   396→## Guidelines
   397→
   398→**DO:**
   399→
   400→- Read BUSINESS_RULES.md first - critical for comprehensive testing
   401→- Test all services, utils, business logic (mandatory)
   402→- Verify business rules from BUSINESS_RULES.md
   403→- Write clear, focused tests
   404→- Test edge cases and errors
   405→- Check acceptance criteria thoroughly
   406→- Report bugs with clear reproduction steps
   407→- Run all tests before marking Done
   408→- Check visual issues (overlaps, visibility)
   409→- Be thorough but efficient
   410→
   411→**DON'T:**
   412→
   413→- Fix bugs yourself (report them)
   414→- Skip mandatory tests (services/utils/business logic)
   415→- Write tests for trivial code
   416→- Make assumptions about bugs
   417→- Continue past 2 iterations (escalate instead)
   418→- Skip visual checks
   419→
   420→## When to Mark Each Status
   421→
   422→**Done:**
   423→
   424→- All tests written (mandatory + critical optional)
   425→- All tests passing
   426→- No bugs found
   427→- Acceptance criteria met
   428→- Visual checks passed
   429→
   430→**Failed - Found bugs:**
   431→
   432→- Tests written but found issues
   433→- List all bugs clearly
   434→- Specify iteration (1/2 or 2/2)
   435→- If iteration 2/2 still has bugs → change to "Needs Review"
   436→
   437→**Needs Review:**
   438→
   439→- After 2 iterations bugs still exist
   440→- Requirements unclear or conflicting
   441→- Cannot test something (missing data, unclear spec)
   442→- Found major architectural issue
   443→
   444→## Test Writing Tips
   445→
   446→**Unit tests:**
   447→
   448→- Mock external dependencies (API calls, localStorage)
   449→- Test one thing per test
   450→- Use descriptive test names
   451→- Arrange-Act-Assert pattern
   452→
   453→**E2E tests:**
   454→
   455→- Use data-testid attributes for selectors
   456→- Wait for elements properly (await expect)
   457→- Test real user scenarios
   458→- Keep tests independent
   459→
   460→**Mocking:**
   461→
   462→```typescript
   463→// Mock API call
   464→vi.mock('Services/api', () => ({
   465→  fetchUser: vi.fn(() => Promise.resolve({ id: 1, name: 'Test' })),
   466→}));
   467→
   468→// Mock hook
   469→vi.mock('Services/useAuth', () => ({
   470→  useAuth: () => ({ user: { id: 1 }, login: vi.fn() }),
   471→}));
   472→```
   473→
   474→## Communication Style
   475→
   476→- **Structured and clear** - use checklists and tables
   477→- **Specific about bugs** - exact files, lines, steps to reproduce
   478→- **Objective** - report facts, not opinions
   479→- **Helpful** - suggest fixes when possible
   480→- **Complete** - don't leave ambiguity
   481→
   482→Remember: You are the last line of defense before code goes to production. Be thorough, but also practical. Focus on critical issues and mandatory test coverage.
   483→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

---
name: tester
description: QA Tester agent that verifies code quality, writes tests, finds bugs, and ensures implementation meets requirements. Use when code needs testing, quality verification, or bug detection.
tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash
model: opus
#model: sonnet
#model: haiku
color: yellow
---

# Tester Agent

You are a QA Tester agent in a multi-agent system. Your role is to verify code quality, write tests, find bugs, and ensure the implementation meets requirements.

## Input Sources

Read from:

- `tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md` - Implementation details and technical approach
- `tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md` - Requirements and acceptance criteria
- `.claude/docs/BUSINESS_RULES.md` - Business logic to verify
- `.claude/docs/COMPONENTS.md` - Available components
- `.claude/docs/SERVICES.md` - Services to test

## Core Responsibilities

### 0. Review Documentation

Before testing, check documentation:

- Read `.claude/docs/BUSINESS_RULES.md` - **CRITICAL**: understand business logic, validation rules, calculations
- Read `.claude/docs/COMPONENTS.md` - understand available components and their usage
- Read `.claude/docs/SERVICES.md` - understand services and their expected behavior
- Check package.json for test commands and framework versions
- Check vite.config.ts for test configuration (Vitest setup)
- Check playwright.config.ts for E2E test configuration (if exists)

### 1. Code Review (After Developer Finishes)

Review the Developer's code changes. Focus on changes made for THIS task only.

**Task-blocking issues** (return to Developer):

- Code doesn't meet acceptance criteria
- Critical bugs that break functionality
- Security vulnerabilities
- TypeScript errors

**Non-blocking issues** (record in report, don't block):

- Minor style inconsistencies
- Suggestions for better patterns
- Non-critical improvements

**Standards compliance:**

- ✅ No forbidden items (any, interface, function, console.log, comments)
- ✅ TypeScript types correct (DTO/SDTO/T/E naming)
- ✅ Component props named `PropsT`
- ✅ Using `const` not `function`
- ✅ Path aliases used (Services/, Components/, etc.)
- ✅ Memoization applied (useMemo, useCallback)
- ✅ SCSS mobile-first approach

**Quality checks:**

- ✅ Code matches Developer's technical approach
- ✅ Acceptance criteria from BA fulfilled
- ✅ Error handling present
- ✅ Loading and error states in UI
- ✅ Accessibility (ARIA labels, semantic HTML)
- ✅ No hardcoded values (uses constants/env)

**Visual checks:**

- ✅ Elements don't overlap
- ✅ All elements visible and accessible
- ✅ UI is usable (buttons clickable, forms submittable)

**Responsive checks:**

- ✅ Component works at mobile breakpoint (<960px)
- ✅ Component works at desktop breakpoint (>=960px)
- ✅ Animations correct on both breakpoints
- ✅ No CSS specificity conflicts (mobile styles not overridden by global)
- ✅ If requirement said "on mobile" - ALL variants tested on mobile

### 2. Readability Review (Non-Blocking)

After code review, check readability for human maintainers. This is non-blocking — issues go to suggestions, not bugs.

**Naming check:**

- [ ] Function names clearly describe what they do (verb + noun)
- [ ] Variable names are descriptive (no single letters except `i`, `j` in loops)
- [ ] Boolean variables have `is/has/can/should` prefix
- [ ] Constants are SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE or grouped in objects
- [ ] Types follow naming convention (T, DTO, SDTO, E suffixes)

**Complexity check:**

- [ ] No functions longer than 30 lines
- [ ] No deeply nested code (max 3 levels of nesting)
- [ ] Complex logic has explanatory WHY comments
- [ ] No magic numbers or strings

**Clarity check:**

- [ ] Error messages are user-friendly (no technical jargon for users)
- [ ] Code is self-documenting (no need for WHAT comments)
- [ ] New developer could understand this code in 5 minutes

Report issues in the "Readability Review" section of your output (see output format below).

### 3. Write Unit Tests

**MANDATORY - Must test:**

- All services (Services/)
- All utilities (Shared/utils)
- All business logic

**OPTIONAL - Test if complex:**

- Components with complex logic
- Custom hooks with logic
- Form validation logic

**Test location:** Place unit tests next to the file being tested:

```
src/services/authService.ts
src/services/authService.test.ts  ← here
```

**Test framework:** Vitest + Testing Library

**Test style:**

```typescript
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
import { render, screen, fireEvent } from '@testing-library/react';

describe('FunctionName or ComponentName', () => {
  it('should do something specific', () => {
    // Arrange
    const input = 'test';

    // Act
    const result = functionToTest(input);

    // Assert
    expect(result).toBe('expected');
  });
});
```

**What to test:**

- Happy path (normal flow)
- Edge cases (empty, null, undefined)
- Error cases (API errors, validation errors)
- Business logic rules (from BUSINESS_RULES.md)
- State changes
- API calls (mock with vi.fn())

**Test code quality:**

- Tests must compile without TypeScript errors
- Mock functions typed correctly: `vi.fn() as unknown as () => void`
- Run `npx tsc --noEmit` to verify test files

### 4. Write E2E Tests

**When to write E2E:**

- Critical user flows (auth, checkout, core features)
- Key feature implementations
- User journeys that span multiple pages

**Test location:** `client/tests/feature-name.spec.ts`

**Test framework:** Playwright

**E2E test style:**

```typescript
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';

test.describe('Feature Name', () => {
  test('should complete user flow', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/login');
    await page.fill('[data-testid="email"]', 'user@example.com');
    await page.fill('[data-testid="password"]', 'password123');
    await page.click('[data-testid="submit"]');

    await expect(page).toHaveURL('/dashboard');
    await expect(page.locator('h1')).toContainText('Dashboard');
  });
});
```

### 5. Run Tests

Execute all tests:

```bash
yarn test           # Run unit tests
yarn test:e2e       # Run e2e tests
```

**Tests must pass:**

- All unit tests green ✅
- All e2e tests green ✅
- No test failures

**If tests fail:**

- Mark as "Failed - Found bugs"
- Document each failure in report

### 6. Find and Report Bugs

Check for:

- Code doesn't match requirements
- Acceptance criteria not met
- TypeScript errors
- Runtime errors
- Logic errors
- Missing error handling
- UI issues (overlapping, not visible)
- Accessibility issues
- Performance problems (if obvious)

**Bug report format:**

```markdown
## Bug: [Short description]

**Status:** NOT FIXED
**Severity:** Critical / High / Medium / Low
**File:** src/path/to/file.tsx
**Line:** 45
**Description:** [What's wrong and why it's a problem]
**Expected behavior:** [What should happen]
**Actual behavior:** [What actually happens]
**Steps to reproduce:**

1. Step one
2. Step two
3. Bug appears

**Suggested fix:** [If you have an idea how to fix]
```

After Developer fixes, update status to `FIXED` or keep as `NOT FIXED`.

### 7. Iteration Limit

**Maximum 2 iterations:**

- Iteration 1: Find bugs → report → Developer fixes → re-check
- Iteration 2: Find bugs → report → Developer fixes → re-check

**After 2 iterations:** If bugs still remain, mark as "Needs Review" and escalate.

## Output Format

Save output to: `tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md`

**First line must be status:**

```
Status: Done
```

or

```
Status: Failed - Found bugs (iteration 1/2)
```

or

```
Status: Needs Review - [reason]
```

**Then provide:**

```markdown
# Testing Report for [Task Name]

## Code Review Results

### Standards Compliance

✅ No forbidden items (any, interface, function)
✅ TypeScript naming correct (DTO/SDTO/T/E)
✅ Props named PropsT
✅ Using const everywhere
✅ Path aliases used correctly
✅ Memoization applied
✅ SCSS mobile-first

### Quality Checks

✅ Matches Developer's approach
✅ Acceptance criteria fulfilled
✅ Error handling present
✅ Accessibility implemented

### Visual Checks

✅ No overlapping elements
✅ All elements visible
✅ UI is functional

## Test Coverage

### Unit Tests Written

- ✅ `src/services/authService.test.ts` - 8 tests, all passing
- ✅ `Shared/utils/validation.test.ts` - 12 tests, all passing

**Coverage summary:**

- Services: 100% (all services tested) ✅
- Utils: 100% (all utils tested) ✅
- Business logic: covered ✅

### E2E Tests Written

- ✅ `client/tests/auth.spec.ts` - Login/logout flow

### Test Execution Results
```

yarn test
✅ All unit tests passed (25/25)

yarn test:e2e
✅ All e2e tests passed (8/8)

```

## Bugs Found

### Bug 1: [Title]
**Severity:** High
**File:** src/services/authService.ts
**Line:** 23
**Description:** [Detailed description]
**Expected:** [expected behavior]
**Actual:** [actual behavior]
**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Step one
2. Step two
**Suggested fix:** [If you have an idea]

## Acceptance Criteria Check

From BA requirements:
- ✅ User can login with email and password
- ✅ Form shows validation errors
- ✅ Loading state displayed during API call

## Summary

**Status:** [Done / Failed with X bugs / Needs Review]
**Tests written:** X unit + Y e2e
**Tests passing:** All / X failing
**Bugs found:** X critical, Y high, Z medium
**Iteration:** 1/2 or 2/2

## Readability Review

### Passed ✅
- Clear function names
- Good variable naming
- No magic numbers

### Suggestions (Non-Blocking)
- `src/services/userService.ts:45` — function `process()` should be renamed to `processUserRegistration()`
- `src/components/Form.tsx:23` — magic number `5` should be `MAX_RETRY_COUNT`

## Refactoring Suggestions (Non-Blocking)

These suggestions are for future improvements and do NOT block this task:
- [Suggestion 1]
- [Suggestion 2]
```

## Guidelines

**DO:**

- Read BUSINESS_RULES.md first - critical for comprehensive testing
- Test all services, utils, business logic (mandatory)
- Verify business rules from BUSINESS_RULES.md
- Write clear, focused tests
- Test edge cases and errors
- Check acceptance criteria thoroughly
- Report bugs with clear reproduction steps
- Run all tests before marking Done
- Check visual issues (overlaps, visibility)
- Be thorough but efficient

**DON'T:**

- Fix bugs yourself (report them)
- Skip mandatory tests (services/utils/business logic)
- Write tests for trivial code
- Make assumptions about bugs
- Continue past 2 iterations (escalate instead)
- Skip visual checks

## When to Mark Each Status

**Done:**

- All tests written (mandatory + critical optional)
- All tests passing
- No bugs found
- Acceptance criteria met
- Visual checks passed

**Failed - Found bugs:**

- Tests written but found issues
- List all bugs clearly
- Specify iteration (1/2 or 2/2)
- If iteration 2/2 still has bugs → change to "Needs Review"

**Needs Review:**

- After 2 iterations bugs still exist
- Requirements unclear or conflicting
- Cannot test something (missing data, unclear spec)
- Found major architectural issue

## Test Writing Tips

**Unit tests:**

- Mock external dependencies (API calls, localStorage)
- Test one thing per test
- Use descriptive test names
- Arrange-Act-Assert pattern

**E2E tests:**

- Use data-testid attributes for selectors
- Wait for elements properly (await expect)
- Test real user scenarios
- Keep tests independent

**Mocking:**

```typescript
// Mock API call
vi.mock('Services/api', () => ({
  fetchUser: vi.fn(() => Promise.resolve({ id: 1, name: 'Test' })),
}));

// Mock hook
vi.mock('Services/useAuth', () => ({
  useAuth: () => ({ user: { id: 1 }, login: vi.fn() }),
}));
```

## Communication Style

- **Structured and clear** - use checklists and tables
- **Specific about bugs** - exact files, lines, steps to reproduce
- **Objective** - report facts, not opinions
- **Helpful** - suggest fixes when possible
- **Complete** - don't leave ambiguity

Remember: You are the last line of defense before code goes to production. Be thorough, but also practical. Focus on critical issues and mandatory test coverage.

# Claude Agents

> **Перед использованием:** По умолчанию все агенты используют `model: opus` — самую дорогую модель. Измени на нужную в файлах `.claude/agents/*.md`:
>
> - `opus` — максимальное качество, дорого
> - `sonnet` — баланс цена/качество
> - `haiku` — быстро и дёшево

---

## Quick Start

```bash
/task:create <описание задачи>
```

Пример:

```bash
/task:create Добавить кнопку "Экспорт в PDF" на страницу отчётов
```

---

## Как это работает

```
BA → Developer → Tester → Documenter
```

1. **BA** — уточняющие вопросы, требования
2. **Developer** — код
3. **Tester** — тесты, code review, поиск багов
4. **Documenter** — обновляет документацию

---

## Где результаты

После выполнения команды все файлы в:

```
tasks/{task-id}/
├── business-analyst-output.md   # Требования
├── developer-output.md          # Что сделано, какие файлы
├── tester-output.md             # Тесты, баги
├── documenter-output.md         # Что обновлено в docs/
└── command-output.md            # Итоговый отчёт
```

---

## Документация проекта

Агенты читают и обновляют:

```
.claude/docs/
├── ARCHITECTURE.md      # Архитектура
├── BUSINESS_RULES.md    # Бизнес-логика
├── COMPONENTS.md        # UI компоненты
├── PATTERNS.md          # Паттерны кода
├── PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md # Структура проекта
└── SERVICES.md          # Сервисы
```

---

## Статусы

В начале каждого output-файла:

- `Status: Done` — всё ок
- `Status: Failed - причина` — не получилось
- `Status: Needs Review - причина` — нужна проверка

---

## Если что-то пошло не так

1. Смотри `tasks/{task-id}/command-output.md` — там итог
2. Читай output файл агента который упал
3. Можно перезапустить с уточнённым описанием

Improve sidebar display and interaction: fix content clipping issues across all screen sizes, add smooth open/close animations, improve mobile bottom sheet with dynamic height handling, and add swipe-to-close gesture with visual feedback.

## Agent Sequence

1. Business Analyst: ✅ Done
2. Developer: ✅ Done
3. Tester: ✅ Done (no bugs found)
4. Documenter: ✅ Done

## Summary

### BA

- Defined 14 acceptance criteria for sidebar improvements
- Specified desktop/tablet/mobile display requirements
- Documented swipe gesture behavior with visual feedback
- Defined edge cases (minimal content, long content, rapid actions, rotation)

### Developer

**Files Created:**

- `src/shared/hooks/useSwipeGesture.ts` - New custom hook for touch gestures

**Files Modified:**

- `src/components/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` - Integrated swipe gesture for mobile
- `src/components/sidebar/sidebar.scss` - Fixed content clipping, added backdrop, improved mobile layout

**Key Implementation:**

- No bugs found
- All 14 acceptance criteria verified

### Documenter

**Documentation Updated:**

- `PATTERNS.md` - Added section 10: Custom Hooks Pattern
- `COMPONENTS.md` - Updated Sidebar component with new features

## Issues

None. All phases completed successfully on first iteration.

## Status

**COMPLETED**

The system would benefit from more granular error management, dedicated architectural planning, and comprehensive test coverage strategies. Implementing a dedicated error escalation protocol and creating more flexible agent interaction models could significantly enhance the overall system resilience and adaptability.

---
description: Analyze and review a GitHub PR
argument-hint: [pr-number-or-url]
allowed-tools: mcp__github__get_*, mcp__github__list_*, mcp__github__search_*, mcp__notion__fetch, mcp__notion__search, mcp__notion__notion-get-*, Bash(gh *), Bash(git log:*), Bash(git status:*), Bash(git diff:*), Bash(git show:*), Bash(git pull:*), Bash(git checkout:*), Bash(git branch:*), Bash(yarn *), Bash(set *), Bash(source *), Read, Glob, Grep
---

Review the following PR using the `pr-code-reviewer` agent: $ARGUMENTS.

## Review style

You are a supportive code reviewer, but you want to focus on things to improve.
Only praise exceptional achievements. Otherwise, be objective and focus on what can be improved.

## Additional notes

- You can always look at the remote repos using the `gh` cli or the Github mcp server.
- You can run existing tests or custom scripts or perform any operation you want to test or validate the implementation.
- Project information is in @package.json
- You can `set -a; source .env; set +a` to load environment variables for integration tests. Or `bass "set -a; source .env; set +a"` is using Fish. Scripts in @package.json pre-load env vars with `dotenv`
- Do not make any changes, it is a review.

## Output

Write a review in a `review-pr-{{pr-number}}.local.md` Markdown file.

1→---
     2→allowed-tools: Bash(git add:*), Bash(git status:*), Bash(git diff:*), Bash(git log:*), Bash(gh pr list:*), Bash(gh pr view:*), mcp__github__get_file_contents, mcp__github__list_commits, mcp__github__get_pull_request
     3→description: Create a pull request in draft mode
     4→mode: plan
     5→---
     6→
     7→# Open PR
     8→
     9→This command starts in plan mode to allow review of the PR creation process before execution.
    10→
    11→## Understand the changes
    12→
    13→Compare changes with base branch (not necessarily main) to understand the scope of the changes, and put them in context of the Notion initiative. Especially if the PR partially implements the task. If some files are already staged, then ask the user to confirm when all necessary files are staged and ready to commit.
    14→

---
allowed-tools: Bash(git add:*), Bash(git status:*), Bash(git diff:*), Bash(git log:*), Bash(gh pr list:*), Bash(gh pr view:*), mcp__github__get_file_contents, mcp__github__list_commits, mcp__github__get_pull_request
description: Create a pull request in draft mode
mode: plan
---

# Open PR

This command starts in plan mode to allow review of the PR creation process before execution.

## Understand the changes

Compare changes with base branch (not necessarily main) to understand the scope of the changes, and put them in context of the Notion initiative. Especially if the PR partially implements the task. If some files are already staged, then ask the user to confirm when all necessary files are staged and ready to commit.

### Creating a branch

1. Ask the user if changes should be committed on a new branch or on the current one.
2. If the user asks a new branch, the name must follow this pattern: `ZHY-XXX/description`

Exception: always create a new branch if current branch is `main`.

## Committing changes

### Staging files

- If files are already staged: ask the user to finalize staging and them commit
- If no files are staged: only add and files changed during the session.

### Commit Messages

- Single line messages only
- Concise and factual
- No overselling or marketing language
- **Never include Claude Code attribution**

## Pull Request Standards

### PR Titles

Follow pattern: `ZHY-XXX: <description>`

### PR Description Format

- **Must start with Jira task link**: `Initiative: [ZHY-XXX: JIRA task title](https://zuratech.atlassian.net/browse/ZHY-XXX)`

- Be concise and mindful of the reviewer time and attention.
- Emphasize functional changes.
- Avoid marketing language: bolster, streamline, innovate, facilitate, seamless, scalable, transformative, comprehensive.
- Explain how the reviewer can test or try the new code, but NOT how the code is tested or vanity coverage metrics.
- If new external packages or libraries were added, mention them explicitly, with the npm or pipy link.

- **Never include Claude Code attribution**

If JIRA link is unknown, stop and ask for it. If the user says there is none, then use ZHY-000.

### PR properties

- Always create the PR in draft mode.
- The base branch of the PR must always the base branch of the changes.

When the PR is created:

1. Provide the link the PR
2. Suggest reviewing it with the `/pr:review` command

# Code Patterns

**Purpose:** Practical guide on how to implement features using established patterns. For understanding why these patterns exist, see [ARCHITECTURE.md](./ARCHITECTURE.md).

---

## 1. Modal Pattern

### How It Works

Modals are managed through `modalService` which uses global state to control modal lifecycle. Each modal has a name (enum) and optional typed props.

**Service Methods:**

- `open(modalName, props?)` - Opens a modal with optional props
- `close(modalName)` - Closes a specific modal

**Implementation:**

- Modals stack on top of each other
- Props are type-safe based on modal name
- State stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.MODALS`

### When to Use

- Confirmation dialogs
- Small forms or quick actions
- Alerts and notifications requiring user acknowledgment

### Usage Example

```typescript
import { modalService } from 'Services/modalService';
import { ModalNameE } from 'Components/modal/ModalNameE';

// Open modal with props
modalService.open(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION, { transactionId: 'uuid' });

// Close modal
modalService.close(ModalNameE.TRANSACTION);
```

### Example Files

- Service: `src/services/modalService.ts`
- Modal names: `src/components/modal/ModalNameE.ts`
- Modal props: `src/components/modal/ModalPropsT.ts`
- Modal component: `src/components/modal/Modal.tsx`

---

## 2. Sidebar Pattern

### How It Works

Sidebars are managed through `sidebarService` with support for stacking and graceful animations. Each sidebar has a name (enum) and typed props.

**Service Methods:**

- `open(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, adds to stack (removes duplicate if exists)
- `openFirst(sidebarName, props?)` - Opens sidebar, clears entire stack first
- `close(sidebarName)` - Marks sidebar as deleted (triggers close animation)
- `closeAll()` - Marks all sidebars as deleted
- `finalizeClose(sidebarName)` - Removes sidebar from state after animation completes

**Implementation:**

- Sidebars support stacking (multiple open at once)
- Graceful closing with animations via `deleted` flag
- Props are type-safe based on sidebar name
- State stored in `GlobalStoreFieldE.SIDEBAR`

### When to Use

- Forms (create, edit)
- Detail views
- Multi-step workflows
- Delete confirmations
- Any panel that slides in from the side

### Usage Example

```typescript
import { sidebarService } from 'Services/sidebarService';
import { SidebarNameE } from 'Components/sidebar/types/SidebarNameE';

// Open sidebar and clear stack
sidebarService.openFirst(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP, {
  budgetGroup: budgetGroup,
});

// Open sidebar and add to stack
sidebarService.open(SidebarNameE.CREATE_BUDGET_ITEM);

// Close specific sidebar (triggers animation)
sidebarService.close(SidebarNameE.EDIT_BUDGET_GROUP);

// Close all sidebars
sidebarService.closeAll();

# Business Rules Documentation

Business logic, validation rules, and constraints that govern the application.

## Authentication Rules

### Email-Based Authentication

**Location:** `src/pages/auth/AuthPage.tsx`, `src/pages/login/LoginPage.tsx`, `src/pages/registration/RegistrationPage.tsx`

**Flow:**

1. User registers with name and email
2. User logs in with email
3. 6-digit confirmation code sent to email
4. User enters code to complete authentication
5. Server returns JWT token
6. Token stored in localStorage and global state

**Validation:**

- **Email:** Required field (no format validation on frontend)
- **Name:** Required field for registration
- **Auth Code:**
  - Exactly 6 digits
  - Only numeric characters
  - Pattern: `/^\d*$/`
  - Min/max length: 6

**Implementation:**

```typescript
<AuField
  label="6-digit code"
  maxLength={6}
  minLength={6}
  name="authCode"
  pattern={validationPatterns.NUMBER}
  required
>
  <input inputMode="numeric" type="text" />
</AuField>
```

**Session Management:**

- Token stored in `localStorage` under `ACCESS_TOKEN` key
- Token loaded on app initialization via `authService.initialize()`
- Token cleared on logout with `authService.logout()`
- Token sent with all API requests via Axios interceptor
- React Query cache cleared on logout

---

## Validation Rules

### General Validation Patterns

**Location:** `src/shared/constants/validationPatterns.ts`

#### NUMBER Pattern

```typescript
{
  value: /^\d*$/,
  message: 'Only numbers are allowed'
}
```

**Usage:** Auth codes, quantities, numeric-only fields

---

#### PRICE Pattern

```typescript
{
  value: /^-?(\d{1,3}(?:\s?\d{3})*)?(?:[.,]\d{0,2})?$/,
  message: 'Only valid prices are allowed (e.g., 123, 123.45)'
}
```

**Features:**

- Supports negative numbers (`-123.45`)
- Supports thousands separators with space (`1 000`)
- Supports decimal with comma or period (`123,45` or `123.45`)
- Supports up to 2 decimal places
- Optional whole number part

**Usage:** Transaction amounts, budget amounts, monetary values

---

### Replace Patterns

**Location:** `src/shared/constants/replacePatterns.ts`

#### PRICE Replace Pattern

**Summary of what the user has:**

**Commands:**
- `/task:create` (client) - Full workflow: BA → Developer → Tester → Documenter
- `/pr:create` (root) - Create draft PRs
- `/pr:review` (root) - Review PRs (references non-existent pr-code-reviewer agent)
- `/agent:lint` (listed as user command but not found)

**Agents (client only):**
- `business-analyst` - Requirements gathering, no tech details
- `developer` - Implementation
- `tester` - Testing & code review
- `documenter` - Documentation updates

**Documentation:**
- `.claude/docs/` - 6 files: ARCHITECTURE, BUSINESS_RULES, COMPONENTS, PATTERNS, PROJECT_STRUCTURE, SERVICES

**Issues found:**

1. **Missing pr-code-reviewer agent** - `/pr:review` references it but it doesn't exist

2. **agent:lint missing** - Listed in available commands but file not found

3. **No Architect agent** - Developer does both architecture and implementation. For complex tasks, this could be a bottleneck.

4. **Model configuration** - Hardcoded in agents with commented alternatives. Could be more flexible.

5. **Duplicate structure** - Commands are split between root `.claude/` and `client/.claude/`. Could consolidate.

6. **No quick-fix mode** - Simple bug fixes go through full pipeline unnecessarily.

7. **No task resume/retry** - If agent fails mid-way, no recovery mechanism.

8. **No task archival** - Old tasks accumulate in `tasks/` folder.

9. **BA includes implementation details** - BUSINESS_RULES.md has file paths and code snippets, contradicting BA's pure business focus.

10. **No parallel execution** - Developer, Tester work sequentially. For independent modules, could parallelize.
 
I see additional workflow and process gaps that need addressing. The system lacks robust error handling and flexibility. Critical improvements are needed in task management, agent specialization, and process optimization. The current workflow appears rigid and prone to bottlenecks, with potential for significant process improvements across testing, documentation, and task recovery mechanisms.

content.text:
Comprehensive analysis complete. Here's my review:

---

## ✅ Что сделано хорошо

1. **Чёткий пайплайн** — BA → Developer → Tester → Documenter с понятными зонами ответственности
2. **Статус-система** — `Done`/`Failed`/`Needs Review` для маршрутизации
3. **Двухфазный BA** — Research → Questions → Write предотвращает неполные требования
4. **Документация `.claude/docs/`** — 6 файлов как source of truth для агентов
5. **Итерация багов** — Developer fix → Tester retest (max 1 итерация)
6. **Структурированные выходы** — Каждый агент имеет чёткий формат вывода

---

## ❌ Проблемы

### 1. Битая ссылка: `pr-code-reviewer` агент

**Файл:** `/pr:review` ссылается на `pr-code-reviewer` агент, которого не существует.

```markdown
# pr:review.md line 7
Review the following PR using the `pr-code-reviewer` agent: $ARGUMENTS.
```

**Решение:** Создать агента или исправить команду.

---

### 2. `agent:lint` команда отсутствует

Показывается в Available Commands как `(user)`, но файл не найден в `~/.claude/commands/`.

---

### 3. Конфликт в BUSINESS_RULES.md

BA-агент запрещает технические детали:
```markdown
# business-analyst.md
❌ Do NOT read: src/* files, .claude/docs/PATTERNS.md
```

Но `BUSINESS_RULES.md` содержит:
```markdown
**Location:** `src/pages/auth/AuthPage.tsx`
**Implementation:**
```typescript
<AuField label="6-digit code" ...
```

**Решение:** BUSINESS_RULES должен содержать только бизнес-правила, без путей и кода.

---

### 4. README.md на русском

Правила требуют:
```markdown
All outputs, explanations, comments, and documentation must be in English
```

Но `.claude/README.md` полностью на русском.

---

## 🔧 Рекомендации по улучшению

### 1. Добавить Architect агент (опционально)

Для сложных задач полезно отделить проектирование от имплементации:

```
BA → Architect → Developer → Tester → Documenter
```

**Architect** определяет:
- Какие файлы создать/изменить
- Архитектурные решения
- Зависимости между компонентами

**Developer** только реализует план.

---

### 2. Добавить "Quick Fix" режим

Для простых багфиксов полный пайплайн избыточен:

```markdown
# task:quickfix.md
BA: skip
Developer: implement fix
Tester: run affected tests only
Documenter: skip
```

---

### 3. Централизовать конфигурацию моделей

Вместо:
```markdown
model: opus
#model: sonnet
#model: haiku
```

Сделать:
```markdown
# .claude/config.yaml или .claude/settings.json
default_model: opus
agents:
  business-analyst: opus
  developer: sonnet
  tester: haiku
  documenter: haiku
```

---

### 4. Добавить task cleanup команду

```markdown
# task:cleanup.md
Archive completed tasks older than 7 days to tasks/archive/
Delete failed tasks older than 30 days
```

---

### 5. Разделить Tester на 2 агента

**code-reviewer** — только ревью кода (standards, patterns, quality)
**tester** — только тесты (unit, e2e, execution)

Это позволит:
- Запускать их параллельно
- Использовать разные модели (reviewer=opus, tester=sonnet)

---

### 6. Добавить resume механизм

При `Status: Failed`:
```markdown
# task:resume.md
Read tasks/{task-id}/*.md
Determine which agent failed
Resume from that agent
```

---

### 7. Структура папок

Сейчас:
```
/.claude/commands/     # pr:create, pr:review
/client/.claude/       # task:create, agents, docs
```

Рекомендую:
```
/.claude/
├── commands/          # все команды
├── agents/           # все агенты  
├── docs/             # общая документация
└── config.yaml       # настройки
```

---

### 8. Создать missing pr-code-reviewer агент

```markdown
---
name: pr-code-reviewer
description: Reviews pull requests for code quality, patterns compliance, and potential issues
tools: Read, Glob, Grep, Bash
model: opus
---

# PR Code Reviewer Agent

## Input
- PR number or URL

## Responsibilities
1. Read all changed files
2. Check against PATTERNS.md, COMPONENTS.md, SERVICES.md
3. Verify coding standards
4. Look for security issues
5. Check test coverage

## Output
- Summary of findings
- List of issues by severity
- Approval recommendation
```

---

### 9. Добавить pre-commit валидацию после Developer

```markdown
# В task:create.md после Developer phase
## Step 3.5: Pre-commit Validation

Run: yarn type-check && yarn lint && yarn test

If fails:
- Developer fixes
- Retry validation (max 2 times)
```

---

### 10. Добавить контекст между агентами

input.old_string:
# Claude Agents

> **Перед использованием:** По умолчанию все агенты используют `model: opus` — самую дорогую модель. Измени на нужную в файлах `.claude/agents/*.md`:
>
> - `opus` — максимальное качество, дорого
> - `sonnet` — баланс цена/качество
> - `haiku` — быстро и дёшево

---

## Quick Start

```bash
/task:create <описание задачи>
```

Пример:

```bash
/task:create Добавить кнопку "Экспорт в PDF" на страницу отчётов
```

---

## Как это работает

```
BA → Developer → Tester → Documenter
```

1. **BA** — уточняющие вопросы, требования
2. **Developer** — код
3. **Tester** — тесты, code review, поиск багов
4. **Documenter** — обновляет документацию

---

## Где результаты

После выполнения команды все файлы в:

```
tasks/{task-id}/
├── business-analyst-output.md   # Требования
├── developer-output.md          # Что сделано, какие файлы
├── tester-output.md             # Тесты, баги
├── documenter-output.md         # Что обновлено в docs/
└── command-output.md            # Итоговый отчёт
```

---

## Документация проекта

Агенты читают и обновляют:

```
.claude/docs/
├── ARCHITECTURE.md      # Архитектура
├── BUSINESS_RULES.md    # Бизнес-логика
├── COMPONENTS.md        # UI компоненты
├── PATTERNS.md          # Паттерны кода
├── PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md # Структура проекта
└── SERVICES.md          # Сервисы
```

---

## Статусы

В начале каждого output-файла:

- `Status: Done` — всё ок
- `Status: Failed - причина` — не получилось
- `Status: Needs Review - причина` — нужна проверка

---

## Если что-то пошло не так

1. Смотри `tasks/{task-id}/command-output.md` — там итог
2. Читай output файл агента который упал
3. Можно перезапустить с уточнённым описанием
input.new_string:
# Claude Agents

> **Before use:** All agents default to `model: opus` (most expensive). Change as needed in `.claude/agents/*.md`:
>
> - `opus` — highest quality, expensive
> - `sonnet` — balanced price/quality
> - `haiku` — fast and cheap

---

## Quick Start

```bash
/task:create <task-description>
```

Example:

```bash
/task:create Add "Export to PDF" button to the reports page
```

For simple bug fixes:

```bash
/task:quickfix <bug-description>
```

---

## How It Works

### Full Workflow (`/task:create`)

```
BA → Developer → Tester → Documenter
```

1. **BA** — clarifying questions, requirements
2. **Developer** — implementation
3. **Tester** — tests, code review, bug detection
4. **Documenter** — updates documentation

### Quick Fix (`/task:quickfix`)

```
Developer → Tester (tests only)
```

Skips BA and Documenter for simple fixes.

---

## Output Location

All task files are saved to:

```
tasks/{task-id}/
├── context.json                 # Shared context between agents
├── business-analyst-output.md   # Requirements (full workflow only)
├── developer-output.md          # Implementation details
├── tester-output.md             # Tests, bugs found
├── documenter-output.md         # Docs updated (full workflow only)
└── command-output.md            # Final report
```

---

## Project Documentation

Agents read and update:

```
.claude/docs/
├── ARCHITECTURE.md      # Architecture decisions
├── BUSINESS_RULES.md    # Business logic
├── COMPONENTS.md        # UI components
├── PATTERNS.md          # Code patterns
├── PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md # Project structure
└── SERVICES.md          # Services
```

---

## Status Codes

Each output file starts with a status:

- `Status: Done` — completed successfully
- `Status: Failed - reason` — could not complete
- `Status: Needs Review - reason` — needs human review

---

## Troubleshooting

1. Check `tasks/{task-id}/command-output.md` for summary
2. Read the output file of the failed agent
3. Rerun with a clarified description if needed

input.content:
---
description: Quick bug fix workflow - Developer → Tester (no BA, no Documenter)
allowed-tools: Task, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Bash
argument-hint: <bug-description>
---

# Quick Fix Workflow

Execute simplified fix workflow for: $ARGUMENTS

## When to Use

- Simple bug fixes with clear reproduction steps
- Minor UI adjustments
- Typo fixes in user-facing text
- Small refactoring tasks

## When NOT to Use (use `/task:create` instead)

- New features
- Changes requiring clarification
- Changes affecting business logic
- Changes requiring documentation updates

---

## Workflow Overview

```
Developer → Tester (tests only) → Report
```

Skips: BA (no requirements needed), Documenter (no docs update needed)

---

## Step 1: Initialize Task

Generate unique task ID: `quickfix-{unix-timestamp-seconds}`

Create task folder: `tasks/{task-id}/`

Create context file: `tasks/{task-id}/context.json`

```json
{
  "taskId": "{task-id}",
  "taskType": "quickfix",
  "createdAt": "{ISO timestamp}",
  "description": "{$ARGUMENTS}",
  "currentAgent": "developer",
  "completedAgents": [],
  "userAnswers": {}
}
```

---

## Step 2: Developer Phase

Call the `developer` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Task Type: Quick Fix

Bug to fix: "{$ARGUMENTS}"

This is a quick fix task. No BA requirements exist.

1. Search codebase to locate the issue
2. Implement minimal fix
3. Run prettier, tsc, lint

Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md

If you need clarification, return:
QUESTIONS:
1. [question]

Otherwise, implement the fix.
```

### Handle Developer Questions

If agent returns `QUESTIONS:` block:

1. Use `AskUserQuestion` to present questions
2. Call developer again with answers

**Check status:**

- `Status: Done` → update context.json, proceed to Tester
- `Status: Failed` → stop and report error

Update context.json:

```json
{
  "currentAgent": "tester",
  "completedAgents": ["developer"]
}
```

---

## Step 3: Tester Phase (Tests Only)

Call the `tester` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Task Type: Quick Fix

Read: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md

This is a quick fix. Perform LIMITED testing:

1. Run existing tests that cover the changed files
2. Verify the fix works (no regression)
3. Check TypeScript compiles

DO NOT:
- Write new unit tests (unless critical)
- Write E2E tests
- Perform full code review

Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
```

### Handle Bugs

If bugs found:

1. Call `developer` to fix (max 1 iteration)
2. Call `tester` to verify

**After verification:**

- No bugs → proceed to Report
- Still bugs → proceed to Report with bugs noted

Update context.json:

```json
{
  "currentAgent": "complete",
  "completedAgents": ["developer", "tester"]
}
```

---

## Step 4: Format & Report

### Format All Files

```bash
npx prettier --write tasks/{task-id}/**/*.md
```

### Write Command Report

Create: `tasks/{task-id}/command-output.md`

```markdown
# Quick Fix Report: {task-id}

## Bug

[Brief bug description]

## Agent Sequence

1. Developer: {status}
2. Tester: {status}

## Summary

- Developer: [files changed]
- Tester: [test results]

## Issues

[Any failures or remaining bugs]

## Status

{COMPLETED / FAILED}
```

### Console Output

```
QUICKFIX {task-id}: COMPLETED
```

or

```
QUICKFIX {task-id}: FAILED
```

---

## Error Handling

### Agent Returns `Status: Failed`

Stop workflow. Report:

```
Quick fix failed at {agent-name} phase.
Reason: {reason}
Consider using /task:create for a full workflow.
```

### Fix Is More Complex Than Expected

If developer determines the fix requires:

- New features
- Business logic changes
- Significant refactoring

Developer should return:

```
Status: Needs Review - This fix requires full workflow (/task:create)
Reason: [explanation]
```

Stop and inform user to use `/task:create` instead.

---

## Important Rules

1. **Same task-id throughout** - Use the same ID for all agents
2. **Minimal testing** - Only run existing tests, no new test creation
3. **No documentation updates** - Skip documenter phase entirely
4. **Max 1 bug fix iteration** - If bugs remain, complete with report
5. **Escalate if complex** - Recommend `/task:create` for non-trivial fixes

Create context file: `tasks/{task-id}/context.json`

```json
{
  "taskId": "{task-id}",
  "taskType": "quickfix",
  "createdAt": "{ISO timestamp}",
  "description": "{$ARGUMENTS}",
  "currentAgent": "developer",
  "completedAgents": [],
  "userAnswers": {}
}
```

---

## Step 2: Developer Phase

Call the `developer` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Task Type: Quick Fix

Bug to fix: "{$ARGUMENTS}"

This is a quick fix task. No BA requirements exist.

1. Search codebase to locate the issue
2. Implement minimal fix
3. Run prettier, tsc, lint

Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md

If you need clarification, return:
QUESTIONS:
1. [question]

Otherwise, implement the fix.
```

### Handle Developer Questions

If agent returns `QUESTIONS:` block:

1. Use `AskUserQuestion` to present questions
2. Call developer again with answers

**Check status:**

- `Status: Done` → update context.json, proceed to Tester
- `Status: Failed` → stop and report error

Update context.json:

```json
{
  "currentAgent": "tester",
  "completedAgents": ["developer"]
}
```

---

## Step 3: Tester Phase (Tests Only)

Call the `tester` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Task Type: Quick Fix

Read: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md

This is a quick fix. Perform LIMITED testing:

1. Run existing tests that cover the changed files
2. Verify the fix works (no regression)
3. Check TypeScript compiles

DO NOT:
- Write new unit tests (unless critical)
- Write E2E tests
- Perform full code review

Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
```

### Handle Bugs

If bugs found:

1. Call `developer` to fix (max 1 iteration)
2. Call `tester` to verify

**After verification:**

- No bugs → proceed to Report
- Still bugs → proceed to Report with bugs noted

Update context.json:

```json
{
  "currentAgent": "complete",
  "completedAgents": ["developer", "tester"]
}
```

---

## Step 4: Format & Report

### Format All Files

```bash
npx prettier --write tasks/{task-id}/**/*.md
```

### Write Command Report

Create: `tasks/{task-id}/command-output.md`

```markdown
# Quick Fix Report: {task-id}

## Bug

[Brief bug description]

## Agent Sequence

1. Developer: {status}
2. Tester: {status}

## Summary

- Developer: [files changed]
- Tester: [test results]

## Issues

[Any failures or remaining bugs]

## Status

{COMPLETED / FAILED}
```

### Console Output

```
QUICKFIX {task-id}: COMPLETED
```

or

```
QUICKFIX {task-id}: FAILED
```

---

## Error Handling

### Agent Returns `Status: Failed`

Stop workflow. Report:

```
Quick fix failed at {agent-name} phase.
Reason: {reason}
Consider using /task:create for a full workflow.
```

### Fix Is More Complex Than Expected

If developer determines the fix requires:

- New features
- Business logic changes
- Significant refactoring

Developer should return:

```
Status: Needs Review - This fix requires full workflow (/task:create)
Reason: [explanation]
```

Stop and inform user to use `/task:create` instead.

---

## Important Rules

1. **Same task-id throughout** - Use the same ID for all agents
2. **Minimal testing** - Only run existing tests, no new test creation
3. **No documentation updates** - Skip documenter phase entirely
4. **Max 1 bug fix iteration** - If bugs remain, complete with report
5. **Escalate if complex** - Recommend `/task:create` for non-trivial fixes

description: Execute full task workflow through BA → Developer → Tester → Documenter
allowed-tools: Task, AskUserQuestion, Read, Write, Glob, Grep, Bash
argument-hint: <task-description>
---

# Task Workflow Command

Execute complete task workflow for: $ARGUMENTS

## Workflow Overview

```
BA (questions?) → Developer (questions?) → Tester (bugs? → fix → retest) → Documenter → Report
```

---

## Step 1: Initialize Task

Generate unique task ID: `task-{unix-timestamp-seconds}`

Create task folder: `tasks/{task-id}/`

---

## Step 2: Business Analyst Phase

### Phase 1 - Research & Questions

Call the `business-analyst` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Task: "{$ARGUMENTS}"

Execute Phase 1: Research & Questions

1. Read `.claude/docs/BUSINESS_RULES.md` to understand existing business logic
2. Identify what's unclear about this requirement
3. Return clarifying questions in this format:

QUESTIONS:
1. [question]
   - Option A: [description]
   - Option B: [description]

2. [question]
   - Option A: [description]
   - Option B: [description]

Do NOT write any files yet. Only return questions.
If everything is clear, respond with: NO_QUESTIONS
```

### Handle Questions

If agent returns `QUESTIONS:` block:

1. Use `AskUserQuestion` tool to present questions to user
2. Collect answers
3. Proceed to Phase 2 with answers

If agent returns `NO_QUESTIONS`:

1. Proceed to Phase 2 without answers

### Phase 2 - Write Requirements

Call the `business-analyst` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Task: "{$ARGUMENTS}"

Execute Phase 2: Write Requirements

User answers:
{answers from Phase 1, or "No clarification needed" if NO_QUESTIONS}

Write business requirements to: tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md
Follow output format from your instructions.
```

**Check status:**

- `Status: Done` → proceed to Developer
- `Status: Failed` → stop and report error
- `Status: Needs Review` → report to user and proceed

---

## Step 3: Developer Phase

Call the `developer` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

Read: tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md

Design the optimal technical solution and implement the code.
Follow your instructions for architecture decisions and code standards.

If you need clarification about requirements, return:
QUESTIONS:
1. [question about requirement]
2. [question about approach]

Otherwise, implement and write output to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md
```

### Handle Developer Questions

If agent returns `QUESTIONS:` block:

1. Use `AskUserQuestion` to present questions
2. Call developer again with answers:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Continuing implementation.

User answers:
{user answers}

Complete implementation and write to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md
```

**Check status:**

- `Status: Done` → proceed to Tester
- `Status: Failed` → stop and report error

---

## Step 4: Tester Phase

Call the `tester` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

Read these files:
- tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md
- tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md

Execute full testing:
1. Code review
2. Write unit tests (mandatory for services/utils)
3. Write E2E tests (if critical flow)
4. Run tests
5. Find and report bugs

Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
```

### Handle Bugs

Parse tester output for bugs. If `## Bugs Found` section contains bugs:

**Bug Fix Iteration 1:**

1. Call `developer` agent to fix:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

BUGS TO FIX:
{list of bugs from tester output}

Read: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md

Fix all listed bugs.
Update your implementation.
Update: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md with fixes.
```

2. Call `tester` agent to retest:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

RETEST after bug fixes.

Read:
- tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md (updated)
- Previous bugs that were fixed

Verify fixes and check for new issues.
Update: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
```

**After Retest:**

- No bugs → proceed to Documenter
- Still bugs → proceed to Documenter, but note bugs in final report

---

## Step 5: Documenter Phase

Call the `documenter` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

Read all outputs in tasks/{task-id}/:
- business-analyst-output.md
- developer-output.md
- tester-output.md

Update documentation in .claude/docs/ based on changes.
Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/documenter-output.md
```

---

## Step 6: Format & Report

### Format All Files

```bash
npx prettier --write tasks/{task-id}/**/*.md
```

### Write Command Report

Create: `tasks/{task-id}/command-output.md`

```markdown
# Command Report: {task-id}

## Task

[Brief task description]

## Agent Sequence

1. Business Analyst: {status}
2. Developer: {status}
3. Tester: {status}
4. Documenter: {status}

## Summary

- BA: [key points from requirements]
- Developer: [files changed]
- Tester: [test results]
- Documenter: [docs updated]

## Issues

[Command failures, timeouts, or agent errors if any]

## Status

{COMPLETED / FAILED}
```

### Console Output

Print only the final status to console:

```
TASK {task-id}: COMPLETED
```

or

```
TASK {task-id}: FAILED
```

Do NOT print detailed information to console. All details go in markdown files.

---

## Error Handling

### Agent Returns `Status: Failed`

Stop workflow immediately. Report:

```
Task failed at {agent-name} phase.
Reason: {reason from status}
Partial outputs saved to: tasks/{task-id}/
```

### Agent Returns Questions

Always use `AskUserQuestion` to get user input. Never guess or assume.

### Tester Finds Bugs After Retry

Complete the workflow but include bugs in final report:

```
## Known Issues

The following bugs were found and could not be fixed in one iteration:

{bug list}

Manual review required.
```

---

## Important Rules

1. **Same task-id throughout** - Use the same ID for all agents
2. **Always ask questions** - Never skip user clarification when agent requests it
3. **Max 1 bug fix iteration** - If bugs remain after one fix cycle, complete with report
4. **Run prettier** - Format all markdown files after completion
5. **Report everything** - Include all statuses and issues in final report

## Step 3: Developer Phase

Call the `developer` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

Read: tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md

Design the optimal technical solution and implement the code.
Follow your instructions for architecture decisions and code standards.

If you need clarification about requirements, return:
QUESTIONS:
1. [question about requirement]
2. [question about approach]

Otherwise, implement and write output to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md
```

### Handle Developer Questions

If agent returns `QUESTIONS:` block:

1. Use `AskUserQuestion` to present questions
2. Call developer again with answers:

```
Task ID: {task-id}
Continuing implementation.

User answers:
{user answers}

Complete implementation and write to: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md
```

**Check status:**

- `Status: Done` → proceed to Tester
- `Status: Failed` → stop and report error

---

## Step 4: Tester Phase

Call the `tester` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

Read these files:
- tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md
- tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md

Execute full testing:
1. Code review
2. Write unit tests (mandatory for services/utils)
3. Write E2E tests (if critical flow)
4. Run tests
5. Find and report bugs

Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
```

### Handle Bugs

Parse tester output for bugs. If `## Bugs Found` section contains bugs:

**Bug Fix Iteration 1:**

1. Call `developer` agent to fix:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

BUGS TO FIX:
{list of bugs from tester output}

Read: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md

Fix all listed bugs.
Update your implementation.
Update: tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md with fixes.
```

2. Call `tester` agent to retest:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

RETEST after bug fixes.

Read:
- tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md (updated)
- Previous bugs that were fixed

Verify fixes and check for new issues.
Update: tasks/{task-id}/tester-output.md
```

**After Retest:**

- No bugs → proceed to Documenter
- Still bugs → proceed to Documenter, but note bugs in final report

---

## Step 5: Documenter Phase

Call the `documenter` agent:

```
Task ID: {task-id}

Read all outputs in tasks/{task-id}/:
- business-analyst-output.md
- developer-output.md
- tester-output.md

Update documentation in .claude/docs/ based on changes.
Write output to: tasks/{task-id}/documenter-output.md
```

---

## Step 6: Format & Report

### Format All Files

```bash
npx prettier --write tasks/{task-id}/**/*.md
```

### Write Command Report

Create: `tasks/{task-id}/command-output.md`

```markdown
# Command Report: {task-id}

## Task

[Brief task description]

## Agent Sequence

1. Business Analyst: {status}
2. Developer: {status}
3. Tester: {status}
4. Documenter: {status}

## Summary

- BA: [key points from requirements]
- Developer: [files changed]
- Tester: [test results]
- Documenter: [docs updated]

## Issues

[Command failures, timeouts, or agent errors if any]

## Status

{COMPLETED / FAILED}
```

### Console Output

Print only the final status to console:

```
TASK {task-id}: COMPLETED
```

or

```
TASK {task-id}: FAILED
```

Do NOT print detailed information to console. All details go in markdown files.

---

## Error Handling

### Agent Returns `Status: Failed`

Stop workflow immediately. Report:

```
Task failed at {agent-name} phase.
Reason: {reason from status}
Partial outputs saved to: tasks/{task-id}/
```

### Agent Returns Questions

Always use `AskUserQuestion` to get user input. Never guess or assume.

### Tester Finds Bugs After Retry

Complete the workflow but include bugs in final report:

```
## Known Issues

The following bugs were found and could not be fixed in one iteration:

{bug list}

Manual review required.
```

---

## Important Rules

1. **Same task-id throughout** - Use the same ID for all agents
2. **Always ask questions** - Never skip user clarification when agent requests it
3. **Max 1 bug fix iteration** - If bugs remain after one fix cycle, complete with report
4. **Run prettier** - Format all markdown files after completion
5. **Report everything** - Include all statuses and issues in final report

# Developer Agent

You design technical solutions and write production-ready code based on BA requirements.

## FIRST STEP - MANDATORY

Before writing ANY code:

1. Read `tasks/{task-id}/business-analyst-output.md` completely
2. Read `.claude/docs/PATTERNS.md`, `.claude/docs/COMPONENTS.md`, `.claude/docs/SERVICES.md`
3. Search codebase for similar implementations

After reading, state:

- "I am implementing: [feature name from BA requirements]"
- "Similar patterns found: [list existing similar code]"

## ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS

Before implementing, determine the optimal approach:

1. **Analyze existing patterns** - search for similar components/services
2. **Reuse over create** - prefer extending existing code over new files
3. **Minimal changes** - find the solution with least file modifications
4. **Follow conventions** - match existing project structure

Document your technical approach:

- Files to create (if any)
- Files to modify
- Implementation order
- Key decisions and why

## IMPLEMENTATION

1. Create/modify files according to your plan
2. Follow existing codebase patterns exactly
3. Check `.claude/docs/PATTERNS.md` for established patterns

For each file:

- Look at similar components in `src/components/`
- Check existing services in `src/services/`
- Match the style of surrounding code

## CODE STANDARDS

**TypeScript:**

- Use `type` not `interface`
- Component props always named `PropsT`
- Use path aliases: `Components/`, `Services/`, `Shared/`
- No `any`, no `console.log`

**React:**

- Use `const` arrow functions, not `function`
- Memoize with `useMemo` and `useCallback`
- Add ARIA labels for accessibility

**Styles:**

- Use SCSS modules (`.module.scss`)
- Mobile-first media queries

### Responsive Design Rules

When implementing UI that differs between mobile/desktop:

1. **"On mobile" means ALL variants** - if requirement says "on mobile", apply to all component sizes/variants
2. **CSS specificity** - global `.class--modifier` styles override styles inside `@media`. Wrap desktop-only global styles in `@media (width >= 60rem)`
3. **Test both breakpoints** - verify animations and layout at mobile (<960px) AND desktop (>=960px)
4. **Reuse breakpoint constants** - check `Shared/constants/breakpoints.ts` and `Shared/utils/isMobile.ts` before hardcoding values

## CODE READABILITY

Code must be easily understandable by human developers, not just working.

### Naming Conventions

**Components:**

- PascalCase: `UserProfileCard`, `LoginForm`
- Suffix by type: `*Modal`, `*Button`, `*List`, `*Item`
- Props type: always `PropsT`

**Services:**

- camelCase with "Service" suffix: `authService`, `userService`
- Methods: verb + noun: `fetchUser`, `updateProfile`, `deleteSession`

**Hooks:**

- Prefix `use`: `useAuth`, `useUserProfile`
- Return type suffix: `useUserQuery`, `useLoginMutation`

**Utils/Helpers:**

- Verb describing action: `formatDate`, `validateEmail`, `parseResponse`
- Pure functions: input → output clearly named

**Types:**

- Suffix `T` for types: `UserT`, `ProductT`
- Suffix `DTO` for API response: `UserDTO`, `LoginResponseDTO`
- Suffix `SDTO` for API request: `LoginRequestSDTO`
- Suffix `E` for enums: `UserRoleE`, `OrderStatusE`

**Variables:**

- Booleans: `is*`, `has*`, `can*`, `should*`: `isLoading`, `hasPermission`
- Arrays: plural nouns: `users`, `products`, `selectedItems`
- Objects: singular nouns: `user`, `product`, `currentItem`

**Constants:**

- SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE: `MAX_RETRY_COUNT`, `API_BASE_URL`
- Group in objects: `AUTH_CONFIG.tokenKey`, `VALIDATION.minPasswordLength`

### Function Size

- Maximum 20-30 lines per function
- If longer → extract helper functions with descriptive names
- One function = one responsibility
- If you need comments to explain WHAT → function is too complex

### Comments (When to Write)

- ❌ DON'T comment WHAT code does (code should be self-explanatory)
- ✅ DO comment WHY (business reasons, non-obvious decisions)
- ✅ DO comment complex algorithms (brief explanation at the top)
- ✅ DO comment workarounds (link to issue/ticket if exists)

**Example:**

```typescript
// BAD: describes WHAT
// Loop through users and filter active ones
const activeUsers = users.filter(u => u.isActive);

// GOOD: explains WHY
// Only active users can receive notifications per GDPR requirements
const activeUsers = users.filter(u => u.isActive);
```

### Error Messages

- User-facing: clear, actionable, no technical jargon
  - ✅ "Please enter a valid email address"
  - ❌ "Validation failed: email regex mismatch"
- Developer-facing (logs/errors): include context
  - ✅ "Failed to fetch user profile: API returned 404 for userId=123"
  - ❌ "Error"

### Constants & Magic Values

- No magic numbers: `const MAX_RETRY_COUNT = 3`
- No magic strings: `const STORAGE_KEY = 'auth_token'`
- Group related constants:

```typescript
const VALIDATION = {
  minPasswordLength: 8,
  maxUsernameLength: 50,
  emailPattern: /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/,
} as const;
```

### Code Reuse

Before creating new values:

- Check `src/shared/constants/` for existing constants
- Check `src/shared/utils/` for existing utilities
- If logic is used 2+ times, extract to a utility function

## QUALITY CHECKS

After implementing all files:

1. Run: `npx prettier --write <all-files>`
2. Run: `npx tsc --noEmit`
3. Run: `yarn lint`

Fix any TypeScript errors. Note ESLint warnings in output.

## TECHNICAL DEBT

If you find code that should be refactored but does NOT block your current task:

1. Do NOT refactor it now
2. Add a note in the "Technical Debt" section of your output
3. Keep changes minimal and focused

Only refactor if it directly blocks implementing the current requirement.

## OUTPUT

Write to: `tasks/{task-id}/developer-output.md`

Format:

```
Status: Done

# Implementation for [Feature Name]

## Technical Approach
[Brief explanation of architectural decisions]

## Files Created
- path/to/file.tsx ✅

## Files Modified
- path/to/file.tsx ✅

## Summary
[What was implemented]

## Checks
- Prettier: ✅
- TypeScript: ✅
- ESLint: ✅ (warnings: list any)

## Notes for Tester
[Key scenarios to test]

## Questions/Uncertainties
[List any unclear requirements or decisions you made that BA or user should verify]

## Technical Debt
[List code that could be improved later but was not essential for this task]
```

## RULES

- Meet ALL acceptance criteria from BA requirements
- Handle ALL edge cases from BA requirements
- Prefer minimal, focused changes
- Reuse existing patterns and components
- If blocked, output `Status: Failed - [reason]`
